title: IBM 'did not adapt' and just lost $70B — is Big Tech (and your portfolio) in trouble? Protect your nest egg now
excerpt: It was a blue chip day for IBM.
content_html:
Thomas Kent

Fri, July 17, 2026 at 7:05 AM CDT 7 min read

Moneywise and Yahoo Finance LLC may earn commission or revenue through links in the content below.

For years, IBM (NYSE: IBM) has been one of the defining names in enterprise technology. But its latest earnings warning shows how quickly artificial intelligence is reshaping even the industry's oldest giants.

IBM stunned investors after preannouncing second-quarter results that fell far short of expectations, sending shares down more than 25% after spending more than $50 billion (1) buying into the AI age with purchases of Red Hat, HashiCorp and Confluent.

Must Read

  • Jeff Bezos backs a platform that lets anyone invest in rental homes for as little as $100 — 6 ways to build wealth like a landlord without actually being one

  • JPMorgan still sees gold hitting $5,000/oz by Q4 — and savvy investors are protecting their wealth with a tax-advantaged Gold IRA. Learn more with a free guide from Priority Gold

  • The tax breaks in Trump's 'big beautiful bill' expire after 2028 — and experts say most people won't act in time. What to do before the window closes

Estimates place the loss at around $70 billion, a 58-year record-breaking loss for the company.

In a letter to investors (2), CEO Arvind Krishna said customers had shifted their quarterly technology budgets toward "servers, storage, and memory purchases" to support AI infrastructure. He acknowledged that "this quarter we faltered" and "did not adapt and move quickly enough."

It's a stark example of a broader shift taking place across corporate America. Rather than simply adding AI spending to existing technology budgets, many businesses are reallocating funds from legacy systems to AI infrastructure and software.

A blue giant

IBM isn't the only major technology company (3) facing difficult questions about how AI will reshape its future.

Salesforce (NYSE: CRM) shares have tumbled this year (1) as investors weigh whether AI agents could disrupt parts of the company's traditional software business. Adobe (NASDAQ: ADBE) has faced similar drops as generative AI tools challenge some of the creative workflows that helped make its products indispensable.

Meanwhile, enterprise software companies such as ServiceNow (NYSE: NOW) and Workday (NASDAQ: WDAY) have also come under pressure.

The circumstances behind each sell-off differ, but together they highlight the same reality. Even dominant technology companies can see their competitive advantages called into question when a major technological shift alters how customers spend their money.

Read More: Millionaires under 43 hold only 25% of their wealth in stocks. Here's where their money is actually going

content_html_zh:
托马斯·肯特

2026 年 7 月 17 日星期五上午 7:05 CDT 阅读时间 7 分钟

Moneywise 和 Yahoo Finance LLC 可以通过以下内容中的链接赚取佣金或收入。

多年来，IBM（纽约证券交易所股票代码：IBM）一直是企业技术领域的定义品牌之一。 但其最新的盈利预警表明，即使是该行业最古老的巨头，人工智能也正在以多么快的速度重塑。

IBM 预先公布的第二季度业绩远低于预期，令投资者震惊，在斥资超过 500 亿美元 (1) 收购 Red Hat、HashiCorp 和 Confluence 进入人工智能时代后，股价下跌了 25% 以上。

必读

  • 杰夫·贝索斯 (Jeff Bezos) 支持一个平台，让任何人都可以以低至 100 美元的价格投资出租房屋——无需真正成为房东即可像房东一样积累财富的 6 种方法
  • 摩根大通仍然预计金价到第四季度将达到 5,000 美元/盎司，而精明的投资者正在通过税收优惠的黄金 IRA 来保护自己的财富。 通过 Priority Gold 的免费指南了解更多信息
  • 特朗普“美丽大法案”中的税收减免将于 2028 年到期，专家表示大多数人不会及时采取行动。 窗口关闭之前可以做什么

据估计，损失约为 700 亿美元，创下该公司 58 年来的亏损纪录。

在致投资者的一封信中 (2)，首席执行官 Arvind Krishna 表示，客户已将季度技术预算转向“服务器、存储和内存采购”，以支持人工智能基础设施。 他承认“这个季度我们步履蹒跚”并且“没有足够快地适应和采取行动”。

这是美国企业界正在发生的更广泛转变的一个鲜明例子。 许多企业不是简单地将人工智能支出添加到现有技术预算中，而是将资金从遗留系统重新分配到人工智能基础设施和软件。

蓝色巨人

IBM 并不是唯一一家面临人工智能将如何重塑未来这一难题的大型科技公司 (3)。

由于投资者权衡人工智能代理是否会扰乱该公司的部分传统软件业务，Salesforce（纽约证券交易所股票代码：CRM）股价今年大幅下跌 (1)。 Adobe（纳斯达克股票代码：ADBE）也面临着类似的下跌，因为生成式人工智能工具挑战了一些有助于使其产品变得不可或缺的创意工作流程。

与此同时，ServiceNow（纽约证券交易所股票代码：NOW）和Workday（纳斯达克股票代码：WDAY）等企业软件公司也面临压力。

每次抛售背后的情况各不相同，但它们共同凸显了相同的现实。 当重大技术变革改变客户花钱方式时，即使是占主导地位的科技公司也会发现自己的竞争优势受到质疑。

了解更多：43 岁以下的百万富翁仅将 25% 的财富投资于股票。 这就是他们的钱实际去向的地方
